qas.q: what is the major focus of the AFMS flight path?
what is a typical tour length for a group commander?
when is a SrA able to serve as a reporting official?
what are the ranks that make uo the SNCO tier?
Whom would the wing commander contact if he or she had a question about the activities and medical resources of the medical unit?
why is it important for a MTF to be designated as a group even if it technically isn't large enough to be formed as a group?
qas.a: to avoid being at a disadvantage compared with other base organizations formed and designates as groups

qas.q: which medical AFSC can serve as the MDG superintendent?
what is the job of the first sergeant?
qas.a: manage unit activities; promoting the welfare, morale, and health of enlisted personnel; maintain discipline

qas.q: what's the min. rank requirement for a squadron superintendent?
which flight member should take the lead if your flight needed to interact w/ another flight to improve overall organ. performance?
on the organizational chart, where is ophthalmology usually found?
qas.a: medical operations squadron under the surgical services flight

qas.q: which flight is responsible for plans and programs, and allocates manpower and funds?
under which squadron is optometry usually found on the chart?
what can you expect as you advance in rank and skill level?
what does the 3rd digit indicate in 4V0X1?
qas.a: career field subdivision; ophthalmic career field

qas.q: how does a person get into the ophthalmic career field?
qas.a: satisfactory completion of the J3ABR4V031 course

qas.q: which publications would you use if you wanted to look up job duties and responsibilities?
what is the main difference between 3- and 5-skill level of 4V0X1?
qas.a: Depth of knowledge; level of job proficiency; responsibility

qas.q: who is responsible for the preparation of pts for ophthalmology treatment?
what is the main difference between 5-(journeyman) and 7- (craftsman) skill level of 4V0X1?
wouls a 9-skill level ophthalmic superintendent be identified as optometry or ophthalmology?
qas.a: neither b/c he or she is at a management level where the tow career fields merge

qas.q: what AF training document identifies life-cycle edu. and training requirements, training support resources, and core task requirements?
where can you find the STS that supervisors and trainers use to conduct unit level training?
what are the 2 distinct parts of the dual-channel concept of EST?
what type of knowledge is needed to perform a particular task safely, accurately, and effectively?
what type of training is the 3-level tech school considered?
why should you ask your supervisor to show you your AFTR?
qas.a: so you know what tasks you need to complete

qas.q: what course must someone need to attend in order to become a certifier and/or a trainer?
why can't your supervisor act as both your trainer and certifier?
what is the main purpose of field evals?
where would you go if you wanted more info on edu. opportunities?
when is an AF member enrolled auto in the CCAF?
qas.a: completion of BMT & assign. to an AFS

qas.q: why can't an airman pick any CCAF degree they want (regardless of their AFSC) ?
qas.a: student must hold the journeyman 5-level in the appropriate AFSC at the time of program completion

qas.q: will you need to take a class to fulfill the physical edu. req. for your CCAF?
qas.a: no b/c the req. is satisfied by the completion of BMT

qas.q: where can you find more info about possibly becoming an optometrist?
why should you get your national certification?
qas.a: can get nominated for awards; looks good on resume for civilian life; supports core values; boosts confidence in job

qas.q: which civilian organization would certify an ophthalmic technician as a CPOT upon completion of exam?
which civilian organization would certify an ophthalmology tech as a COA upon completion of exam?
what military provision came about b/c of Public Law 91-596, known as OSHAct of 1970?
qas.a: eye protection for all employees working in hazardous environments

qas.q: when a high-ranking visitor tours an eye haxardous area, is he or she requires to wear eye protection?
qas.a: yes b/c everyone who enters hazardous areas are required to wear the proper protection

qas.q: Who will have to pay for an employees exam if he or she need a current prescription for the proper eye wear protection?
qas.a: the employee b/c OSHA doesn't require employers to pay for their employees eye exams

qas.q: who should you contact for the most up-to-date vision conservation and readiness program info?
qas.a: check with local base safety office and bioenvironmental engineering at your MTF

qas.q: which laser workers require medical surveillenace?
qas.a: class 3B and 4 lasers b/c they are at great risk of injury due to such exposures

qas.q: when do the laser workers who require medical surveillance need to be seen ?
qas.a: before initial assignment and upon termination of duties involving lasers

qas.q: what should happen if there is an ocular exposure/ suspected exposure to a laser?
qas.a: immediate involvement of the local flight surgeon; Tests include: operational & medical history, external examination, best cc DVA & NVA, amsler grid, pupil exam, stereopsis, color vision, slit lamp eval, retinal exam, oct if available

qas.q: when is PPE required to be used?
qas.a: when there are hazards that can do bodily harm through absorption, inhalation, or physical contact

qas.q: who pays for the inserts for employee who wears glasses/ CL and requires a full-face piece respirator for their job?
can employees wear CL if there is a possibility that they can be exposed to vapors or gas?
qas.a: no b/c the substance can permeate and get trappen in lens or even btw lens and eye itself

qas.q: who should you contact of you need help identifying which PPE to use for a particular hazardous task?
if you are not a retrainee, what is the minimum time you must be in UGT before getting your 5-skill level?
who carries the CEM code and normally consults with and advises the associate chief on enlisted issues, concerns, and career field matters?
what does qual. training increase?
what should you consider to be the final authority for doing daily tasks?
what is the typical tour length for a squadron commander?
what is the 3rd leading cause of blindness?
what are examples of laser personnel?
what changes can cause the acceptable practice and beliefs of a medical professional to become unacceptable?
what is stated to be of great significance in the Hippocratic Oath?
what is meant by beneficence?
qas.a: the act of bringing about good, or to act in the best interest of someone else

qas.q: What decisions must be made with distributive justice and why?
qas.a: which pt will receive what treatment, how often, and how many pt can be treated b/c there is limited resources

qas.q: what must all outpatient records contain?
qas.a: info to identify pt, support diagnoses, justify treatment, and accurately document the results of care given

qas.q: what section or the pt health record would you file a visual field and exam form received from a refractive surgery center?
what documents from sources external to the MTF must be scanned and imported into AHLTA?
qas.a: specialty car & urgent care results, emergency room notes, discharge summary reports, laboratory results, radiology reports provided by the health care organization and provider

qas.q: can a pt get a copy of something in their medical records?
qas.a: yes b/c the pt has the right to their info according to HIPAA of 1996 & the Privacy Act of 1974

qas.q: who is the custodian for outpatient and inpatient health records?
What do you do is AHLTA is down and you need to document info from a pt's visit?
who is financially accountable for all care delivered to their enrollees, both in ant out of their facility?
Which system is primarily used to enter referrals?
who should the doctor direct the referral to if they want to send a pt off base to a specialist?
qas.a: RMC for coordination with the network provider

qas.q: what counts as a clinic visit?
qas.a: any walk in or appointment where pt has been treated, examined, etc

qas.q: what activity does RMO, MAJCOM, & Headquarters USAF perform that makes It important for them to know how many pts you see vs the staff you have?
what does proper documentation of a visit consist of?
qas.a: S(ubjective) O(bjective) A(ssessment) P(lan) & a signed, dated entry that states specifically what you did for the pt

qas.q: how frequently will verification occur if RMO verifies pt count 3 months in a row with no errors?
what VA would require a flyer to need GMI?
what VA would require a nonflyer to need GMI?
which type of pt would you need to refer to the AF Form 469 more often?
what does the AF Form 496 need to say ehile member is on steroid eye drops after CRS?
qas.a: duty limitations; reflect their current mobility status to commander

qas.q: What is the min DNIF time for a pilot just returning from having LASIK?
what can the reports in both the CRS & ACSCLP programs in ASIMS help you track?
qas.a: which members have/have not made their mandatory apt

qas.q: what is the name of the form used when ACLP member reports CL related corneal abrasions and where can you find it ?
qas.a: ACLP incident report form; found in the ACLP section in ASIMS

qas.q: who can you reach out to for assistance if you having difficulty getting an ACLP member to make their mandatory apt?
what level do field publications originate from?
what info do you need to provide to the librarian to order a civilian publication?
qas.a: stock #, descr. (title, author, edition, publisher), unit cost, quantity, justification

qas.q: What 7 steps from the Tongue & Quill should we incorporate in our OIs ?
qas.a: analyze purpose & audience, research topic, support ideas, organize & outline, draft, edit, fight for feedback & get approval

qas.q: what is the name of the system that the record custodian will use to maintain all electronic records?
who appoints the ophthalmic clinic property custodian?
when the property custodian finds equipment or supplies missing or damaged, who should they notify?
what are 3 types of justice?
what is a health record used for ?
how often should audit verification be done if clinic has an error rate over 10%?
AF publications fall under which classes?
when is a supplement issued?
qas.a: if a MAJCOM would like to clarify or add to an AF publication

qas.q: what is the best way to formally coordinate an OI?
qas.a: initiate a staff summary sheet to circulate through all concerned sections

qas.q: what fint should you right the OIs?
what personnel with the responsibility for overseeing records management would you most likely run into ?
what must happen if property custodian is absent from account for 46+ days ?
what list or form shows all property charged out to the eye clinic account?
what form should be used if an item is not present during inventory?
what are the categories of supply items?
qas.a: medical or nonmedical, consumable or durable

qas.q: what is the max level of consumable supplies for each section of the hospital?
who can approve the purchase of expense equip. for the section?
where is a request for hospital equip. prioritized & tentatively approved?
who inspects equipment being turned-in to determine its serviceability?
what is the primary purpose of SPD?
qas.a: contribute to improves pt care by providing sterile & non-sterile supplies & equip. efficiently & economically across the med facility

qas.q: what are 3 ways sterile package contents can become contaminated?
qas.a: moisture can seep in, seal can break, dirt or dust forced through package pores

qas.q: why are sterile supplies stored in a limited-access, enclosed area?
qas.a: minimize traffic through area & help reduce the # of contamination and excessive air movement

qas.q: how does a positive-pressure ventilation system help maintain a sterile storage area?
why is it important to control the temp & humidity of sterile storage area?
qas.a: can change integrity of package & make them unsterile

qas.q: how far away from the ceilings, floors, and walls should sterile items be stored?
what type of material aids in cleaning & should be used for storage shelves?
how often are sterile supply storage areas cleaned and checked for outdates?
what basic rule do you follow when handling sterile supply items ?
qas.a: handle them as little as possible

qas.q: when so you check the integrity of a sterile package?
qas.a: before use, when removing item from storage, putting item in storage, & immediately after sterilization

qas.q: when is a dust cover placed on a steam-sterilized item & why ?
qas.a: after it has cooled to room temp; to prevent moisture from getting to them

qas.q: what are commercially packages items?
what are 3 packaging characteristics you should consider when determining shelf life?
qas.a: permeability; length pkg retains barrier capability; length of time considered sterile

qas.q: what type of packaging should you use if your items require material that is less affected by repeated handling?
which level does safeguarding public property under control of the AF apply?
what do you do prior to taking over an equipment account?
qas.a: conduct a physical inventory of equipment

qas.q: who would approve authority to purchase a new $65,000 retinal camera?
how much does an investment equipment cost?
what is a function of the SPD?
qas.a: use acceptable methods & techniques for processing materials

qas.q: what is a service that the SPD provides ?
